title: FoodHaus Fellowship for Action 2026–2027: Fully Immersive 9-Month Global Fellowship Empowering Changemakers to Transform Food Systems
summary: Applications are now open for the second cohort of the FoodHaus Fellowship for Action, a transformative 9-month fellowship designed for individuals committed to creating a more just, sustainable, and resilient food system. The program brings together passionate changemakers from diverse professional backgrounds and equips them with the knowledge, leadership skills, networks, and practical tools needed to turn innovative ideas into meaningful action. Hosted by FoodHaus, the Fellowship for Action offers participants a unique opportunity to deepen their understanding of food systems while developing real-world projects and interventions that address pressing challenges in food production, distribution, accessibility, sustainability, and community well-being. Whether you are a farmer, chef, scientist, artist, educator, social entrepreneur, policymaker, activist, social worker, development professional, or simply someone passionate about food systems transformation, this fellowship provides a supportive environment to learn, collaborate, and lead impactful change. Early Bird and Scholarship Deadline Application Deadline for Early Bird Registration and Scholarship Consideration: July 1, 2026 Interested applicants are encouraged to submit their applications before the deadline to maximize their chances of securing available scholarship support. About the FoodHaus Fellowship for Action The FoodHaus Fellowship for Action is specifically designed for individuals who believe that food can serve as a powerful catalyst for social, environmental, and economic transformation. Over a period of nine months, fellows will participate in an immersive learning journey that combines: Systems thinking Collaborative leadership development Personal growth and self-awareness Practical project design and implementation Community engagement International peer learning The fellowship recognizes that creating meaningful change within food systems requires more than technical knowledge. It also demands strong leadership, relationship-building skills, personal resilience, and the ability to work collaboratively across sectors and cultures. Through a carefully structured curriculum, participants will explore food systems challenges, identify leverage points for change, and design initiatives capable of generating lasting impact within their communities, organizations, or regions. Program Duration The fellowship runs from: October 2026 to August 2027 During this period, fellows will engage in a variety of online and in-person learning experiences that support both personal and professional development. What Fellows Will Experience 1. Weekly Online Learning Sessions Participants will take part in regular virtual sessions throughout the fellowship period. These sessions provide opportunities to: Explore complex food system challenges Learn systems-thinking methodologies Participate in collaborative discussions Develop leadership capabilities Share experiences with fellow changemakers Receive guidance from experts and mentors The online format allows participants from different parts of the world to engage consistently while maintaining their existing professional and community commitments. 2. Personal Coaching Support One of the distinctive features of the fellowship is individualized coaching. Participants will receive personal coaching that helps them: Clarify their goals and vision Navigate challenges and uncertainties Strengthen leadership capacity Build confidence in their change-making journey Maintain momentum throughout the program This personalized support ensures that each fellow can develop their unique pathway toward impact. 3. Two In-Person Intensive Gatherings in Switzerland The fellowship includes two immersive residential experiences held on a biodynamic farm in Switzerland. These in-person intensives provide opportunities to: Build meaningful relationships with fellow participants Engage in hands-on learning experiences Deepen personal reflection and leadership development Explore regenerative and biodynamic agricultural practices Strengthen collaborative project work Connect theory with practice in a real-world setting The residential format creates a powerful environment for community-building and transformative learning. 4. Access to a Global Community Fellows become part of an international network of: Mentors Practitioners Food systems innovators Project collaborators Social entrepreneurs Community leaders Alumni This global community creates opportunities for long-term partnerships, collaboration, knowledge sharing, and continued impact beyond the fellowship period. Who Should Apply? The Fellowship for Action is ideal for individuals who: Want to deepen their understanding of food systems and move toward action. Seek clarity about their role in driving food systems transformation. Have an idea, initiative, project, or intervention they want to develop further. Value personal growth alongside professional development. Believe collaboration is essential for creating systemic change. Are excited to learn from people with diverse cultural and professional backgrounds. Want to create positive impact within their organizations, communities, or regions. Applicants do not need to come from a traditional food-sector background to participate. Benefits of the FoodHaus Fellowship for Action Participants will gain access to numerous benefits, including: Leadership Development Strengthen collaborative leadership skills. Develop confidence as a changemaker. Learn how to navigate complexity and uncertainty. Food Systems Understanding Build a deeper understanding of interconnected food systems. Identify leverage points for meaningful intervention. Explore sustainable and regenerative approaches. Project Development Transform ideas into actionable initiatives. Receive structured guidance throughout the design process. Develop strategies for implementation and impact. International Network Join a diverse cohort of global changemakers. Build relationships across sectors and disciplines. Access future collaboration opportunities. Personal Growth Increase self-awareness and resilience. Clarify personal values and purpose. Develop relational skills necessary for long-term change. Diverse Backgrounds Welcome FoodHaus intentionally welcomes participants from a broad range of professional and lived experiences. Potential participants may include: Social entrepreneurs Farmers Chefs Policymakers Artists and creatives Educators Activists Scientists Community organizers Social workers Development practitioners Nonprofit professionals Public sector professionals Students and emerging leaders The fellowship values diversity of perspective and encourages applications from anyone committed to advancing a more equitable and sustainable food future. Scholarship Opportunities To increase accessibility and participation, FoodHaus is offering a limited number of scholarships for selected participants. Applicants seeking financial support should ensure they submit their applications before the scholarship deadline. Why This Fellowship Matters Food systems influence nearly every aspect of society, including health, climate resilience, economic opportunity, social justice, biodiversity, and community well-being. Addressing these interconnected challenges requires leaders who can think systemically, collaborate effectively, and take meaningful action. The FoodHaus Fellowship for Action aims to cultivate exactly these kinds of leaders by combining practical learning, personal transformation, global collaboration, and real-world impact. For individuals passionate about creating positive change through food, this fellowship represents an exceptional opportunity to develop the skills, networks, and confidence needed to move from vision to action. Click here for more and to apply Final Thoughts The FoodHaus Fellowship for Action 2026–2027 offers an exceptional platform for changemakers who believe food can be a powerful driver of social and environmental transformation. Through systems thinking, leadership development, coaching, global networking, and hands-on learning experiences in Switzerland, participants will gain the tools and support necessary to create meaningful and lasting change. If you are ready to turn your vision into action and contribute to a more just and sustainable food system, this fellowship could be the ideal next step in your leadership journey. title: USC POIR Predoctoral Summer Institute 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for First-Generation Students Exploring PhD Programs in Political Science and Social Sciences
summary: For many first-generation students, pursuing a PhD can feel like navigating unfamiliar territory. Questions about graduate school applications, funding opportunities, research expectations, and academic careers often remain unanswered due to limited access to mentorship and professional networks. To help bridge this gap, the University of Southern California (USC) is offering the USC POIR Predoctoral Summer Institute for First-Generation Scholars 2026, a fully funded academic development program designed to support aspiring scholars interested in pursuing doctoral studies in political science, international relations, and related social science disciplines. Hosted by USC s Department of Political Science and International Relations (POIR), the three-day institute will take place from July 8–10, 2026 , providing participants with direct access to faculty mentors, current PhD students, application guidance, and invaluable insights into the world of doctoral education. Best of all, the program is completely free and covers travel, accommodation, and meals for selected participants. What is the USC POIR Predoctoral Summer Institute? The USC POIR Predoctoral Summer Institute is an intensive academic enrichment program specifically designed to support first-generation undergraduate, master s, and law students who are considering pursuing a PhD. The initiative aims to demystify the graduate school process and provide participants with the tools, mentorship, and confidence needed to successfully apply to doctoral programs. Through interactive workshops, mentorship sessions, networking opportunities, and application guidance, participants gain firsthand exposure to the realities of doctoral education and academic careers. The program particularly seeks to strengthen pathways into academia for students who may not have access to traditional mentoring networks or family experience with graduate education. Program Dates Predoctoral Summer Institute July 8–10, 2026 Application Deadline June 15, 2026 Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications as early as possible before the deadline. Why USC Created This Program According to Professor Christian Grose, co-founder of the Predoctoral Summer Institute, the program was created to expand opportunities for first-generation scholars and increase representation within doctoral education and academia. The institute recognizes that many talented students: Excel academically Demonstrate strong research potential Have an interest in scholarly careers However, many lack access to: Graduate school mentors Information about PhD applications Knowledge of funding opportunities Understanding of academic career pathways The institute addresses these barriers by providing structured support and direct access to experienced scholars. Who Should Apply? The USC POIR Predoctoral Summer Institute welcomes applications from: Eligible Participants First-generation undergraduate students First-generation master s students First-generation law students Applicants should be interested in pursuing doctoral studies in: Political Science International Relations Sociology Economics Public Policy Other related social science disciplines While the program primarily serves students in the Western United States, especially Southern California, students from diverse backgrounds are encouraged to apply. What Participants Will Learn The institute provides a comprehensive introduction to doctoral education and academic careers. Participants will gain insights into: Understanding PhD Programs Structure of doctoral education Research expectations Coursework requirements Academic career pathways Graduate School Admissions Application strategies Personal statements Research statements Writing samples Curriculum vitae (CVs) Academic resumes Funding Opportunities Fellowships Graduate assistantships Scholarships Research funding options Academic Research Careers Life as a PhD student Conducting research Publishing scholarly work Teaching and academic service Key Components of the Institute 1. Navigating PhD Applications One of the most valuable aspects of the institute is direct guidance on doctoral applications. Participants receive: Detailed application advice Admissions insights Strategies for building competitive applications Examples of successful PhD application materials Attendees will also have access to actual application materials used by students admitted to top doctoral programs in political science. 2. Mentorship and Personalized Support Participants work directly with: USC faculty members Current PhD students Academic mentors Mentors provide feedback on: Research statements Personal statements CVs and resumes Writing samples Graduate school goals These personalized sessions help participants strengthen their future applications. 3. Connecting with Scholars The institute creates opportunities for meaningful engagement with experienced researchers and academics. Participants can: Discuss research interests Explore academic pathways Learn about ongoing research Build professional networks These interactions provide valuable insights into doctoral life and future academic careers. 4. USC Application Fee Waiver Selected participants receive an additional benefit: USC POIR PhD Application Fee Waiver This helps reduce financial barriers associated with applying to graduate school. Fully Funded Benefits The USC POIR Predoctoral Summer Institute is completely free for participants. The program covers: Travel Costs Participants receive travel support to attend the institute. Accommodation Housing is provided during the program. Meals Room and board are fully covered. Training and Mentorship All workshops, mentorship sessions, and learning activities are offered at no cost. This ensures that financial limitations do not prevent talented students from participating. Why This Opportunity Matters First-generation students often face unique challenges when navigating graduate education. Many students: Lack exposure to academic career pathways Have limited access to mentorship Are unfamiliar with doctoral application processes Face financial and informational barriers The USC POIR Predoctoral Summer Institute directly addresses these challenges by providing structured support and access to experienced scholars who can help participants prepare for future success. How to Apply Interested applicants should complete the official application form before the deadline. Application Deadline June 15, 2026 Applications submitted by the deadline will receive full consideration. Apply Here Why You Should Apply If you are a first-generation student considering a future in research, academia, public policy, international affairs, or the social sciences, the USC POIR Predoctoral Summer Institute offers an exceptional opportunity to gain clarity, mentorship, and confidence. Participants leave the program with: A deeper understanding of doctoral education Stronger application materials Valuable mentoring relationships Expanded academic networks Greater confidence in pursuing a PhD For aspiring scholars seeking a pathway into academia, this fully funded institute could be the first step toward a transformative academic journey. For more information, visit the official website Discover more fully funded PhD program opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post USC POIR Predoctoral Summer Institute 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for First-Generation Students Exploring PhD Programs in Political Science and Social Sciences appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Save the Children USP Disability Rights and Inclusion Fellow (Guyer) 2026: Paid Remote Fellowship Opportunity in the United States for Students and Recent Graduates
summary: Save the Children US is inviting applications for the prestigious USP Disability Rights and Inclusion Fellow (Guyer) position, a paid remote fellowship designed for students and recent graduates who are passionate about disability rights, inclusive development, child wellbeing, and social impact. This fellowship offers a unique opportunity to contribute to meaningful initiatives that improve the lives of children and families living in under-resourced rural communities across the United States. Through hands-on engagement with disability inclusion strategies, research, stakeholder engagement, program development, and knowledge management, fellows will gain valuable professional experience while contributing to one of the world s most respected child-focused organizations. Made possible through the David Guyer Fellowship, this opportunity allows emerging professionals to work directly with experts and practitioners dedicated to ensuring equitable access to education, nutrition, health services, and emergency support for children with disabilities. About Save the Children For more than a century, Save the Children has championed the rights and wellbeing of children around the world. The organization believes every child deserves: A healthy start in life Access to quality education Protection from harm A safe environment to grow and thrive Equal opportunities regardless of circumstance Operating in more than 100 countries, including the United States, Save the Children delivers life-changing programs focused on child protection, education, health, nutrition, emergency response, and humanitarian assistance. By joining Save the Children, fellows become part of a global movement committed to creating positive and lasting change for children and communities. About the USP Disability Rights and Inclusion Fellowship The USP Disability Rights and Inclusion Fellow will support Save the Children’s efforts to advance disability rights and inclusion across its United States Programs. These programs serve children and families living in underserved and under-resourced rural communities through initiatives focused on: Early childhood development School-age learning Nutrition Emergency preparedness Emergency response Child wellbeing programs The fellow will work closely with multiple teams across the organization, including: Program Quality and Impact Monitoring, Evaluation, Research, and Learning (MERL) State Implementation Teams Head Start Programs Disability Rights and Inclusion Specialists The role is designed to help mainstream disability inclusion across existing programs while also identifying opportunities for targeted disability-focused interventions. Fellowship Objectives The fellowship seeks to ensure that children with disabilities have equitable access to services, opportunities, and support systems that enable them to thrive. Key objectives include: Advancing disability rights within Save the Children s U.S. programs. Promoting inclusive programming and service delivery. Supporting evidence-based decision-making. Strengthening partnerships with disability-led organizations. Enhancing organizational learning and knowledge management. Expanding resource mobilization opportunities for disability-inclusive programming. Key Responsibilities The fellowship provides broad exposure to disability inclusion work within a leading nonprofit organization. Cross-Functional Program Support (20%) Under the guidance of the U.S. Disability Rights and Inclusion Advisor, fellows will support teams working with children and families in rural communities throughout the United States. Activities may include: Program coordination Technical support Research assistance Inclusion planning Stakeholder engagement Research and Landscape Analysis (20%) Fellows will conduct: Desk research Literature reviews Policy analyses Landscape assessments Funding opportunity scans Research topics may include: Disability inclusion practices Public policy developments Education accessibility Health equity Child protection Inclusive programming approaches Development of Inclusive Resources (20%) The fellow will contribute to reviewing and developing: Toolkits Training materials Reports Presentations Internal guidance documents Communication materials These resources will support disability-inclusive programming across Save the Children’s U.S. operations. Knowledge Management (15%) Responsibilities include: Organizing shared resources Documenting lessons learned Capturing promising practices Supporting organizational learning initiatives Maintaining knowledge repositories Partnerships and Stakeholder Engagement (15%) The fellow will support engagement with: Disability-led organizations Community-based organizations Educational institutions Schools Advocacy groups Local collaborators These partnerships help strengthen disability inclusion efforts across communities. Communications Support (5%) Fellows will help develop content highlighting program impact through: Success stories Social media content Talking points Impact narratives Meeting and Training Coordination (5%) Additional responsibilities include supporting: Workshops Trainings Working groups Internal meetings External stakeholder sessions Professional Development Benefits One of the most attractive aspects of this fellowship is the extensive professional development support available to participants. Hands-On Experience Fellows gain direct exposure to high-impact nonprofit and humanitarian initiatives. Structured Learning Opportunities Participants receive access to: Subject Matter Expert (SME) trainings Professional development workshops LinkedIn Learning courses Networking events Social mixers Mentorship Fellows benefit from mentorship and guidance from experienced leaders in: Humanitarian response Nonprofit management Disability inclusion Child development Public policy Leadership Exposure Participants may have opportunities to: Present findings to leadership teams Share recommendations Contribute to strategic discussions Networking and Collaboration Opportunities Save the Children places a strong emphasis on collaborative learning. Fellows will: Build relationships with professionals from diverse backgrounds. Engage with mission-driven colleagues. Connect with fellow interns and fellows. Participate in an inclusive professional community. Flexible Remote Work Environment The fellowship is fully remote and designed to support flexibility while maintaining meaningful engagement and productivity. Benefits include: Flexible work arrangements Outcome-focused culture Collaborative team structures Opportunities to contribute innovative ideas Supportive and inclusive workplace environment Compensation and Fellowship Details Position Type Fellowship Location Remote – United States Compensation $17.00 – $17.95 per hour (depending on location) Working Hours Maximum of 24 hours per week Fellowship Duration September 28, 2026 – December 11, 2026 Why You Should Apply This fellowship offers an exceptional opportunity to: Develop expertise in disability rights and inclusion. Gain practical nonprofit experience. Work with one of the world s leading child-focused organizations. Build professional networks. Strengthen research and policy analysis skills. Contribute to meaningful change for children and families. Receive mentorship from sector leaders. Enhance future career opportunities in development, education, health, policy, and social impact sectors. Commitment to Child Protection and Safety Save the Children maintains a strong commitment to child safeguarding and protection. All representatives of the organization are prohibited from engaging in activities that could result in child abuse or exploitation. The organization also prioritizes employee safety, providing staff and fellows with training, resources, and support to maintain secure working environments. Application Information Interested applicants should prepare their application materials and submit them through Save the Children’s official recruitment platform. Candidates requiring disability-related accommodations during the application process may request assistance through the organization’s recruitment support services. Given the competitive nature of Save the Children fellowships, applicants are encouraged to submit strong applications that clearly demonstrate their commitment to disability inclusion, child wellbeing, and social impact. Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Fellowship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post Save the Children USP Disability Rights and Inclusion Fellow (Guyer) 2026: Paid Remote Fellowship Opportunity in the United States for Students and Recent Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: 2026–2027 Hillary Rodham Clinton Research Fellowship: Fully Funded Opportunity for Emerging Women, Peace and Security Researchers
summary: The Georgetown Institute for Women, Peace and Security (GIWPS) has announced applications for the prestigious 2026–2027 Hillary Rodham Clinton Research Fellowship, a highly competitive one-year fellowship designed to support emerging scholars and researchers working at the intersection of gender, peace, security, and international affairs. This unique fellowship offers recent Master s and PhD graduates, as well as final-semester graduate students, the opportunity to conduct rigorous, policy-oriented research that addresses critical knowledge gaps within the Women, Peace and Security (WPS) field. The fellowship is hosted by the Georgetown Institute for Women, Peace and Security, one of the world s leading institutions dedicated to advancing women s leadership and participation in peacebuilding, conflict prevention, and global security. For aspiring researchers seeking to influence policy, contribute to global peacebuilding efforts, and develop expertise in gender-responsive security frameworks, this fellowship represents an exceptional opportunity. About the Georgetown Institute for Women, Peace and Security (GIWPS) The Georgetown Institute for Women, Peace and Security is a renowned institution based in Washington, D.C., dedicated to promoting a more peaceful, equitable, and just world through research, policy engagement, and advocacy. GIWPS works to: Advance the role of women in peace and security processes. Generate evidence-based research on gender and conflict. Influence national and international policy. Strengthen global understanding of the Women, Peace and Security agenda. Support initiatives that prevent conflict and foster sustainable peace. Through its research and policy work, the institute collaborates with governments, international organizations, academics, civil society organizations, and development practitioners around the world. About the Hillary Rodham Clinton Research Fellowship The Hillary Rodham Clinton Research Fellowship is a one-year research program created to support emerging scholars who are committed to advancing knowledge and policy solutions related to Women, Peace and Security. The fellowship provides participants with the opportunity to: Conduct high-quality policy-oriented research. Address significant evidence and knowledge gaps in the WPS field. Contribute to research that informs decision-makers and policymakers. Work within a leading international research institute. Engage with experts, practitioners, and policymakers. Strengthen their professional and academic careers. The fellowship reflects the commitment of GIWPS to advancing evidence-based solutions that promote gender equality, peacebuilding, and security worldwide. Research Focus Areas Selected fellows will conduct research related to Women, Peace and Security (WPS), with emphasis on addressing pressing policy questions and generating actionable insights. Potential areas of interest may include: 1. Gender and Conflict Research examining how armed conflict affects women and girls, including: Gender-based violence Displacement and migration Protection challenges Women s resilience during crises 2. Women’s Leadership in Peacebuilding Studies exploring: Women s participation in peace negotiations Inclusive governance Community peacebuilding initiatives Leadership development 3. Security and International Policy Research focusing on: National security frameworks International peace and security policies Global governance mechanisms Women’s inclusion in security institutions 4. Fragile and Conflict-Affected Settings Research investigating: State fragility Humanitarian crises Recovery and reconstruction Social cohesion and resilience 5. Women, Peace and Security Policy Frameworks Analysis of: National Action Plans on WPS International commitments United Nations frameworks Regional security initiatives Benefits of the Fellowship The Hillary Rodham Clinton Research Fellowship offers numerous professional and academic advantages. Research Opportunities Fellows will: Conduct meaningful and impactful research. Contribute to policy discussions. Develop expertise in Women, Peace and Security. Professional Development Participants can: Build relationships with leading scholars and practitioners. Gain exposure to policymaking processes. Enhance research and analytical skills. Career Advancement The fellowship can help participants: Strengthen academic credentials. Expand professional networks. Increase competitiveness for future academic, policy, and international development positions. Access to a Global Network Fellows will become part of a community of experts working to advance peace, security, and gender equality worldwide. Who Should Apply? This fellowship is ideal for individuals who are passionate about: Gender equality Peacebuilding International security Human rights Conflict prevention Evidence-based policymaking International development Candidates should possess strong research abilities and a commitment to producing work that can influence policy and practice. Eligibility Criteria To be eligible for the 2026–2027 Hillary Rodham Clinton Research Fellowship, applicants must meet the following requirements: Academic Requirements Applicants must: Be in the final semester of an advanced degree program (Master s or PhD), OR Have completed an advanced degree within the previous three years. Knowledge Requirements Applicants should have: Working knowledge of the Women, Peace and Security agenda. Familiarity with relevant U.S. and international policy frameworks. Professional and Academic Background Candidates should demonstrate experience or academic training related to: Gender studies International affairs Peace and conflict studies Security studies Development studies Related disciplines Particular interest is given to individuals whose work focuses on conflict-affected and fragile settings. GIWPS Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion The Georgetown Institute for Women, Peace and Security strongly values diversity, equity, and inclusion. GIWPS believes that diverse perspectives strengthen efforts to create a more peaceful, equitable, and just world. The institute welcomes applications from individuals of all backgrounds and actively encourages diverse candidates to apply. The organization maintains a strong commitment to: Equity Inclusion Respect Representation Diversity of experiences and perspectives GIWPS does not discriminate based on: Race Ethnicity National origin Gender identity and expression Sexual orientation Disability Age Religious beliefs Socioeconomic status Other protected identities The institute emphasizes creating an environment where all individuals feel valued, respected, and empowered to contribute their unique talents and perspectives. Why This Fellowship Matters The Women, Peace and Security agenda has become increasingly important in addressing modern global challenges. Conflicts, humanitarian crises, and fragile governance systems continue to disproportionately affect women and girls worldwide. The Hillary Rodham Clinton Research Fellowship provides emerging researchers with a platform to generate evidence that can help shape policies, improve outcomes for vulnerable populations, and strengthen peacebuilding efforts globally. By supporting innovative and policy-relevant research, the fellowship contributes to a growing body of knowledge that informs governments, international organizations, and civil society actors working toward sustainable peace and gender equality. How to Apply Interested applicants should visit the Georgetown Institute for Women, Peace and Security careers page and locate the 2026–2027 Hillary Rodham Clinton Research Fellowship application. Applicants are encouraged to carefully review all application requirements and submit their materials before the application window closes. Application Link: https://giwps.georgetown.edu/about/careers/ Institution: Georgetown Institute for Women, Peace and Security (GIWPS) Location: Washington, D.C., United States Duration: One Year Deadline: Not specified Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Fellowship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post 2026–2027 Hillary Rodham Clinton Research Fellowship: Fully Funded Opportunity for Emerging Women, Peace and Security Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: ACU Fellowships 2026: Funding for Academic and Professional Staff to Build Global Research Partnerships Across the Commonwealth
summary: The Association of Commonwealth Universities (ACU) has officially opened applications for the 2026 ACU Fellowships, offering academic and professional staff from ACU member universities the opportunity to develop international collaborations, exchange expertise, and strengthen partnerships across the Commonwealth. Designed to promote the sharing of knowledge, skills, and innovative ideas, ACU Fellowships support research visits lasting between one and six months at an ACU member university located in a different country from the applicant s home institution. The fellowship programme aims to foster meaningful international partnerships, encourage collaborative research, and contribute to the advancement of higher education throughout the Commonwealth. Whether you are an academic researcher, finance specialist, policy expert, university administrator, or professional services staff member, the ACU Fellowships provide a valuable opportunity to expand your professional network, enhance your expertise, and contribute to solving global challenges through international cooperation. Application Deadline Important Dates Applications Open: 28 April 2026 Applications Close: 25 June 2026 at 3:00 PM UTC Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications well before the deadline to avoid last-minute technical issues. About the ACU Fellowships The ACU Fellowships programme supports collaboration between universities across the Commonwealth by enabling staff members to undertake research and professional exchange visits at partner institutions. The fellowships are designed to: Promote international collaboration. Facilitate knowledge exchange. Strengthen institutional partnerships. Encourage innovative research. Build professional capacity. Support the development of solutions to shared global challenges. Through these fellowships, participants gain access to new perspectives, research environments, expertise, and networks that can lead to long-term collaborations and future opportunities. Fellowship Duration Successful applicants can undertake research or professional exchange visits lasting: Minimum: 1 month Maximum: 6 months Visits must take place at an ACU member university located in a different country from the applicant s employing institution. Available ACU Fellowship Opportunities 1. Accountancy Fellowship Supported by the Worshipful Company of Chartered Accountants in England and Wales The Accountancy Fellowship seeks to strengthen financial sustainability within higher education institutions across the Commonwealth. Award Value One fellowship available. Funding of up to £2,500 GBP. Fellowship Objectives The fellowship aligns with the ACU Higher Education Taskforce s Finance and Funding Working Group and aims to: Strengthen financial resilience in higher education. Encourage collaboration among finance and funding experts. Support evidence-based policymaking. Connect academics and policymakers. Promote long-term sustainability of higher education institutions. Share best practices in university funding and financial management. Eligible Project Themes Applicants must propose projects related to one of the following areas: Higher Education Finance Projects exploring financial systems, budgeting strategies, resource allocation, and financial management within universities. Research-to-Policy Engagement Projects focused on translating research findings into practical policy recommendations and improving collaboration between researchers and policymakers. Sustainable and Innovative Funding Models Projects examining new approaches to financing higher education institutions and improving financial sustainability. 2. Hong Kong Inbound and Outbound Fellowships Supported by the Hong Kong Jockey Club The Hong Kong Inbound and Outbound Fellowships are designed to strengthen collaboration and mobility between universities in Hong Kong and institutions across the Commonwealth. Award Value Two fellowships available. Funding of up to £2,500 GBP per fellowship. Fellowship Categories Hong Kong Inbound Fellowship Supports travel to Hong Kong from another Commonwealth country. Hong Kong Outbound Fellowship Supports travel from Hong Kong to another Commonwealth member country. Fellowship Objectives These fellowships aim to: Promote international academic mobility. Encourage collaborative research. Strengthen institutional partnerships. Facilitate cross-border knowledge exchange. Build sustainable international networks. Reporting Requirement All fellowship recipients will be required to submit a report upon completion of their fellowship activities. Benefits of the ACU Fellowships Successful fellows will gain access to a wide range of professional and academic benefits. International Collaboration Opportunities Participants will: Build partnerships with experts across the Commonwealth. Develop collaborative research projects. Create long-term professional relationships. Professional Development Fellows can: Enhance their research capabilities. Gain exposure to new methodologies and practices. Learn from international experts. Access to New Resources and Facilities Participants may gain access to: Advanced research facilities. Specialized laboratories. Institutional resources. Academic networks. Career Advancement The fellowship experience can: Strengthen professional credentials. Expand international experience. Improve future funding and collaboration prospects. Knowledge Exchange Fellows will have opportunities to: Share expertise. Exchange innovative ideas. Contribute to institutional capacity building. Eligibility Criteria To apply for an ACU Fellowship, applicants must meet the following requirements. Employment Requirement Applicants must be: Academic staff, or Professional services staff at an ACU member university. Collaboration Requirement Applicants must: Identify a collaborator at an ACU member university in another country. Establish contact with the collaborator before applying. Develop a proposed project or collaboration plan. The host institution must be located in a different country from the applicant s employing university. How to Apply Step 1: Identify a Collaborator Applicants must first identify and approach a collaborator at an ACU member university in another Commonwealth country. Step 2: Review Guidance Materials Before applying, applicants should carefully review: Application Guidance documents. Fellowship FAQs. Eligibility requirements. Step 3: Create or Access a MyACU Account Applicants must apply through the MyACU portal. Existing users can log in and access the application form. New users must register for a MyACU account before accessing the application system. Step 4: Submit the Online Application Click here for more and to apply Fellow Success Story Past fellowship recipients have highlighted the significant impact of the programme on their careers and research development. One such example is Atiqah Aziz from the University of Malaya, who collaborated with Swansea University through an ACU Fellowship. During the fellowship, she participated in collaborative cancer research, received mentoring from leading academics, gained access to advanced laboratory facilities, expanded her professional network, and developed skills that opened doors to further opportunities. Her experience demonstrates how ACU Fellowships can create lasting academic and professional benefits while fostering impactful international collaborations. Why You Should Apply The ACU Fellowships programme offers far more than travel funding. It provides a platform for building international partnerships, developing innovative research, strengthening higher education systems, and advancing professional growth across the Commonwealth. For academic and professional staff seeking to expand their global networks, gain international experience, and contribute to meaningful institutional and societal impact, the ACU Fellowships 2026 represent an outstanding opportunity. Application Deadline Reminder Applications close on 25 June 2026 at 3:00 PM UTC . Interested applicants should begin identifying collaborators and preparing their proposals as early as possible to maximize their chances of success. Click here for more global fellowship opportunities The post ACU Fellowships 2026: Funding for Academic and Professional Staff to Build Global Research Partnerships Across the Commonwealth appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: ACEP Next Generation Resource Governance Leaders Program 2026: Fully Immersive Pan-African Internship for Future Leaders in Governance, Energy, and Sustainable Development
summary: The Africa Centre for Energy Policy (ACEP) has officially announced applications for the 11th Cohort of its highly respected Next Generation Resource Governance Leaders Program (NextGen Leaders Program), a transformative internship initiative designed to equip young Africans with the knowledge, practical skills, and leadership capabilities required to address complex governance challenges across the continent. As Africa continues to navigate critical issues related to natural resource management, energy transition, extractive industries, economic transformation, and sustainable development, the need for a new generation of ethical, innovative, and competent leaders has never been greater. The Next Generation Resource Governance Leaders Program was established to respond to this need by developing a pipeline of talented young Africans committed to advancing good governance and sustainable development throughout Africa. The program provides participants with a unique opportunity to combine theoretical learning with practical experience, ensuring that interns gain both academic insights and real-world exposure to governance challenges and solutions. About the Africa Centre for Energy Policy (ACEP) The Africa Centre for Energy Policy (ACEP) is widely recognized as a leading policy think tank and thought leader in Africa’s extractive governance sector. The organization works to promote: Economic transformation Transparency and accountability Inclusive development Sustainable resource management Effective governance systems Responsible energy sector development Through its research, policy advocacy, stakeholder engagement, and capacity-building initiatives, ACEP has played a significant role in shaping discussions around natural resource governance in Africa. The Next Generation Resource Governance Leaders Program represents one of ACEP’s flagship capacity development initiatives aimed at preparing future African leaders to address emerging governance challenges. What is the Next Generation Resource Governance Leaders Program? The Next Generation Resource Governance Leaders Program is an intensive internship and leadership development initiative designed to provide young Africans with multidisciplinary training, practical exposure, and research opportunities. The program goes beyond traditional internships by creating a structured learning environment where participants can develop both technical expertise and leadership competencies. The initiative balances: Academic and theoretical learning Practical workplace experience Research development Leadership training Professional growth Career advancement preparation Participants are encouraged to think creatively and develop innovative solutions to governance challenges affecting Africa and other developing regions. Objectives of the Program The program seeks to achieve several strategic goals, including: 1. Building a Pipeline of Future African Leaders One of the primary objectives of the program is to nurture a generation of young African leaders capable of driving positive change in governance and public policy. 2. Promoting Good Governance Through specialized training and practical exposure, participants gain a deeper understanding of governance systems and their role in promoting transparency, accountability, and sustainable development. 3. Developing Professional Competencies The program helps participants acquire practical skills needed to excel in professional environments and leadership positions. 4. Strengthening Research Capacity Participants are trained to conduct evidence-based analysis and develop innovative approaches to solving governance challenges. 5. Encouraging Ethical Leadership The program emphasizes ethical leadership principles, preparing participants to lead with integrity, responsibility, and accountability. Key Areas of Training The NextGen Leaders Program offers a comprehensive curriculum covering multiple areas critical to Africa’s development. Resource Governance in Africa and Beyond Participants will gain extensive knowledge on: Natural resource governance Extractive industries management Energy policy Transparency mechanisms Public accountability systems Sustainable resource utilization Transformational Leadership The leadership component focuses on: Leadership principles Change management Strategic thinking Decision-making skills Team leadership Public service ethics Professional and Career Development Participants will receive guidance on: Career planning Professional growth strategies Workplace effectiveness Personal branding Networking skills Career management Digital Communication Skills Modern governance increasingly depends on effective communication. Interns will therefore develop competencies in: Digital engagement Online communication Information dissemination Strategic messaging Content development Technology Skills Participants will also strengthen their digital literacy and technological competencies, which are increasingly essential in contemporary professional environments. What Participants Will Gain The program is specifically designed to equip participants with the tools necessary to thrive in today s competitive labor market. Some of the expected outcomes include: Enhanced Leadership Capacity Participants will develop stronger leadership abilities and learn how to inspire positive change within their communities and organizations. Improved Problem-Solving Skills The program encourages innovative thinking and equips interns with frameworks for addressing complex governance issues. Practical Professional Experience Interns will gain real-world experience that complements their academic backgrounds. Research Skills Development Participants will improve their ability to conduct policy analysis, research, and evidence-based decision-making. Career Readiness The internship prepares participants to transition successfully into professional careers across various sectors. Expanded Professional Networks Interns will have opportunities to interact with professionals, policymakers, researchers, and fellow young leaders from across Africa. Why the Program Matters for Africa Africa faces numerous governance-related challenges, including: Resource management issues Economic transformation concerns Institutional weaknesses Transparency gaps Sustainable development challenges Addressing these issues requires a new generation of capable leaders equipped with modern knowledge, practical skills, and ethical values. The Next Generation Resource Governance Leaders Program contributes directly to this vision by investing in Africa s future leadership. The Structured Capacity Development Approach Unlike many conventional internship programs, ACEP s NextGen Leaders Program follows a structured capacity development model. This approach ensures that participants receive: Systematic training Continuous mentorship Practical assignments Leadership coaching Research opportunities Professional development support The ultimate goal is to create well-rounded professionals capable of making meaningful contributions to governance and development across the continent. Application Components Interested applicants should carefully review the following sections before submitting their applications: Eligibility Criteria Responsibilities of NextGen Leaders What ACEP Offers Required Application Documents Application Essay Questions Language of Instruction Selection Process Application Procedures Applicants should ensure that all required materials are prepared and submitted according to the official application guidelines. How to Apply Interested candidates should visit the official application portal and complete the required application process. Applicants are encouraged to carefully prepare their application essays and supporting documents to maximize their chances of selection. Since this is the 11th cohort of a highly competitive leadership development program, early preparation is strongly recommended. Final Thoughts The ACEP Next Generation Resource Governance Leaders Program 2026 presents an exceptional opportunity for young Africans seeking to build careers in governance, public policy, sustainable development, energy, natural resource management, and leadership. By combining rigorous training, practical experience, research opportunities, and leadership development, the program empowers participants to become transformative leaders capable of addressing Africa’s most pressing governance challenges. For aspiring leaders committed to ethical governance, sustainable development, and positive change across Africa, the NextGen Leaders Program represents a valuable stepping stone toward a meaningful and impactful career. Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Internship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post ACEP Next Generation Resource Governance Leaders Program 2026: Fully Immersive Pan-African Internship for Future Leaders in Governance, Energy, and Sustainable Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Fully Funded PhD Position in Just and Inclusive Urban Mobility Transition at Delft University of Technology (TU Delft), Netherlands 2026
summary: Explore How Sustainable Urban Mobility Can Be Made Fair, Inclusive, and Accessible for All Applications are now open for an exciting fully funded PhD position at Delft University of Technology (TU Delft) in the Netherlands. The doctoral research project, titled “Shaping a Just and Inclusive Urban Mobility Transition,” offers a unique opportunity for aspiring researchers to contribute to one of the most pressing challenges facing cities today: creating sustainable mobility systems that are socially equitable and spatially inclusive. As cities around the world grapple with climate change, urban congestion, environmental degradation, and increasing social inequalities, the need for sustainable transportation solutions has become more urgent than ever. However, mobility transitions are not merely technological transformations. They also involve important questions about fairness, accessibility, housing, spatial planning, and social justice. This PhD project seeks to address these challenges by exploring how principles of mobility justice and spatial accessibility can guide future urban mobility transitions, ensuring that both the benefits and burdens of sustainable mobility are distributed fairly across different communities and geographic areas. About the Research Project Urban mobility plays a critical role in everyday life. It determines how people access: Employment opportunities Educational institutions Healthcare services Public facilities Social networks and recreational activities Despite its importance, access to transportation and mobility opportunities is not equally distributed. Many communities face barriers due to: Geographic location Economic circumstances Limited transportation infrastructure Social inequalities Housing and land-use patterns As governments and cities increasingly promote sustainable transportation solutions such as active mobility, shared mobility, public transportation, and digital accessibility, concerns regarding fairness and inclusivity become increasingly important. The PhD project aims to investigate how mobility transitions can be designed and implemented in ways that ensure equitable outcomes for all population groups. Why This Research Matters Current mobility policies often focus on environmental sustainability and technological innovation. However, they sometimes overlook the social consequences of these transitions. For example: Who benefits from new mobility policies? Which communities may be disadvantaged? How do planning decisions affect vulnerable populations? Can sustainable mobility solutions be equally accessible across different neighborhoods? How can digital accessibility reduce transportation disadvantages? These are the types of critical questions that this doctoral research seeks to answer. The project will contribute to both: Academic knowledge on mobility justice and spatial accessibility. Practical tools and strategies for planners, policymakers, designers, and municipalities. Potential Research Themes The successful PhD candidate will enjoy substantial flexibility in shaping their own research agenda within the broader theme of mobility justice and sustainable urban transitions. Possible research directions include: 1. Inclusive Active and Shared Mobility Solutions Research may examine how active transportation and shared mobility services can be adapted to different social and spatial contexts. Potential areas include: Cycling infrastructure Walking accessibility Shared mobility systems Micromobility solutions Community-based transportation initiatives 2. Citizen and Stakeholder Perspectives The project may explore how residents, communities, and stakeholders perceive justice and fairness in mobility transitions. Research topics may include: Public participation Community engagement Decision-making processes Perceptions of transportation equity 3. Mobility, Housing, and Spatial Planning Mobility systems are deeply connected to urban development and housing policies. Potential research areas include: Land-use planning Affordable housing accessibility Urban expansion Transit-oriented development Spatial inequalities 4. Equity Impacts of the 15-Minute City Concept The increasingly popular 15-minute city model seeks to ensure that essential services can be reached within a short distance. Research may investigate: Accessibility benefits Equity implications Social inclusion outcomes Spatial distribution of services 5. Digital Accessibility and Transport Disadvantage Digital technologies are increasingly changing how people access services and opportunities. Potential research topics include: Remote work Online public services Digital inclusion Transport disadvantage reduction Hybrid accessibility models Research Methodology The project is expected to employ a mixed-methods research approach, combining qualitative and quantitative techniques. Methods may include: Case studies Stakeholder interviews Community engagement workshops Participatory research methods Surveys Accessibility analysis Spatial analysis Fieldwork will be conducted in one or more locations across the Netherlands. The research may focus on: Urban areas Suburban environments Metropolitan regions Peri-urban communities What TU Delft Is Looking For TU Delft is seeking a highly motivated and critically engaged researcher who is passionate about creating sustainable and inclusive mobility futures. Applicants should possess: A Master’s degree in a relevant discipline. Interest in sustainable mobility transitions. Commitment to social and spatial justice. Strong analytical and research skills. Interest in participatory and community-engaged research. Excellent communication abilities. Strong English language proficiency. Dutch language proficiency at CEFR B2 level or higher. Funding and Benefits The selected PhD candidate will receive a fully funded four-year employment contract. Employment Structure The appointment includes: Initial 1.5-year contract Progress evaluation after 15 months Additional 2.5-year contract upon successful evaluation Salary The monthly gross salary increases throughout the PhD trajectory: First Year: €3,059 per month Fourth Year: €3,881 per month Additional Benefits Successful candidates will also receive: 8% holiday allowance 8.3% end-of-year bonus Enrollment in the TU Delft Graduate School Access to doctoral training programs Professional development opportunities Flexible working arrangements Health insurance discounts Monthly work-cost contributions Academic mentorship and supervision Relocation Support International candidates relocating to the Netherlands will benefit from: Relocation guidance Settlement support services Networking events Dual Career Programme support for accompanying partners About Delft University of Technology Delft University of Technology (TU Delft) is among Europe’s leading universities in science, engineering, design, and innovation. The university is internationally recognized for its contributions in areas such as: Climate action Sustainable energy Urban development Mobility innovation Digital technologies Biotechnology The Faculty of Architecture and the Built Environment is globally renowned for its excellence in design-oriented research and interdisciplinary collaboration. The successful candidate will join the Urban Studies section within the Department of Urbanism, where researchers investigate the interactions between people, urban environments, and societal transformations. Application Requirements Applicants must submit the following documents: 1. Detailed Curriculum Vitae (CV) A comprehensive CV in English. 2. Motivation Letter A cover letter in English outlining: Motivation for applying Research interests Suitability for the position Maximum length: 2 pages. 3. Research Proposal Maximum 700 words (excluding references). The proposal should include: Problem statement Research questions Research design Methodology Expected outcomes 4. Academic References Names and contact information of two academic referees. Reference letters are not required at the application stage. Important Dates Application Deadline 21 June 2026 (Local Dutch Time) Interview Date 9 July 2026 CLICK HERE TO APPLY For aspiring researchers interested in sustainable cities, mobility justice, urban planning, and inclusive transportation systems, this fully funded PhD position at TU Delft presents an outstanding opportunity to conduct impactful research while contributing to more equitable and sustainable urban futures. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Job Opportunities on OFY here . The post Fully Funded PhD Position in Just and Inclusive Urban Mobility Transition at Delft University of Technology (TU Delft), Netherlands 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Commonwealth Fund Harkness Fellowships in Health Care Policy and Practice 2027–2028: Fully Funded Global Fellowship for Health Policy Leaders
summary: The Commonwealth Fund has officially opened applications for the prestigious 2027–2028 Harkness Fellowships in Health Care Policy and Practice , an exceptional international fellowship program designed for mid-career professionals committed to transforming health care systems and improving health outcomes. The Harkness Fellowship offers a unique opportunity for emerging and established health policy leaders to conduct innovative research, engage with leading experts, and contribute to policy reforms that improve health care access, quality, efficiency, and equity. Through this highly competitive fellowship, participants undertake a policy-oriented research project with relevance to both the United States and their home country while expanding their professional networks and leadership capacity. Applications for the 2027–2028 cohort are open from June 1, 2026, to November 1, 2026 . About the Commonwealth Fund Harkness Fellowship The Harkness Fellowship is one of the world s most respected international health policy fellowships. It is administered by The Commonwealth Fund , a private foundation dedicated to promoting a high-performing health care system that achieves better access, improved quality, and greater efficiency, particularly for society s most vulnerable populations. The fellowship is specifically designed for professionals who have demonstrated excellence in health policy and practice and who have the potential to become influential leaders in shaping the future of health care systems. Fellows spend time developing and implementing a research project that addresses pressing health policy challenges and contributes to meaningful policy or system-level change. Fellowship Objectives The program focuses on advancing research and leadership in areas that align with The Commonwealth Fund s key priorities, including: Improving health insurance coverage Expanding access to health care services Enhancing quality of care Increasing efficiency in health care delivery Advancing health equity Supporting evidence-based health policy reforms Strengthening health care systems through innovative solutions The fellowship aims to equip participants with the skills, knowledge, and international perspective needed to address complex health care challenges in their home countries and beyond. Benefits of the Harkness Fellowship Successful fellows gain access to numerous professional and academic opportunities, including: 1. International Research Experience Participants conduct a high-impact policy-oriented research project with direct relevance to both the United States and their home country. 2. Access to Global Health Policy Experts Fellows engage with leading policymakers, researchers, academics, clinicians, and health care executives across international health systems. 3. Leadership Development The fellowship is designed to strengthen leadership capabilities and prepare participants for senior roles in health policy and health system transformation. 4. Professional Networking Opportunities Participants become part of a distinguished global network of Harkness alumni, health policy experts, and decision-makers. 5. Policy Influence Research projects are specifically designed to contribute to policy discussions and drive system-level improvements in health care delivery and governance. 6. Cross-Country Learning The fellowship encourages comparative learning between the United States and participating countries, allowing fellows to identify transferable solutions and best practices. Who Can Apply? To be eligible for the 2027–2028 Harkness Fellowship, applicants must satisfy all of the following requirements: Residency Requirement Applicants must be established residents of one of the following countries: Australia Canada France Germany The Netherlands New Zealand Norway Singapore United Kingdom Applicants must also be eligible to receive a J-1 Research Scholar Visa and be committed to continuing their careers in the country from which they are applying. Professional Experience Candidates should be mid-career professionals with significant accomplishments and demonstrated leadership potential in health policy or health care practice. Professionals may come from diverse sectors, including: Government Public policy Health services research Clinical practice Health care management Journalism Health administration Public health organizations Academic institutions Demonstrated Expertise Applicants must possess: Strong expertise in health policy issues Evidence of influencing policy or practice Experience conducting research, policy analysis, or health system leadership activities A proven record of professional achievement Research Project Requirement Applicants must propose a research project that: Addresses important health policy issues Is relevant to both the United States and their home country Focuses on policy or system-level change Demonstrates feasibility and practical impact Educational and Professional Qualifications Candidates must have the educational background and professional experience necessary to successfully complete the proposed project and maximize the fellowship experience. Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion The Commonwealth Fund strongly encourages applications from: Historically marginalized communities Underrepresented populations in health policy Underrepresented groups in health care leadership Professionals bringing diverse perspectives to health system reform This commitment reflects the fellowship s broader goal of promoting health equity and inclusive leadership across health care systems. Application Components Applicants must complete their submission through the Harkness Fellowship Portal and provide the following materials: 1. Statement of Professional Objectives A detailed statement outlining career goals, leadership aspirations, and reasons for applying. 2. Curriculum Vitae (CV) An updated professional resume highlighting qualifications, achievements, publications, and relevant experience. 3. Preliminary Project Proposal Applicants must submit a policy-oriented project proposal of up to five pages (excluding references). The proposal should: Address a significant health policy issue Demonstrate relevance to both countries Outline methodology Explain expected policy impact Highlight opportunities for system-level change 4. Three Letters of Reference Applicants must secure: One Institutional or Organizational Reference from a supervisor or senior leader within their organization Two Professional References from individuals who can assess their qualifications, achievements, and leadership potential Applicants are advised to request references as early as possible to ensure timely submission. 5. Work Samples Candidates must submit three examples of professional work, which may include: Journal articles Research papers Policy reports Technical reports Published articles Other professional writing samples For unpublished materials, applicants should provide a brief explanation describing the work and its purpose. Application Review and Selection Process Applications undergo a rigorous review process conducted by Commonwealth Fund staff and expert review panels in each participating country. Selection is based on: Professional qualifications Leadership potential Commitment to improving health care Quality of the proposed research project Policy relevance of the research Strength of recommendation letters Potential to influence health policy and practice Following initial review, shortlisted applicants may be invited to participate in interviews. Interview Locations Depending on the country of application, interviews may be conducted virtually or in person in: Sydney (Australia) Toronto (Canada) Paris (France) Berlin or The Hague (Germany and the Netherlands) Auckland (New Zealand) Oslo (Norway) Singapore London (United Kingdom) Interview details will be communicated directly to shortlisted candidates. Important Notes Applicants should note that: Fellowship awards cannot be used toward academic degree programs. The fellowship cannot be undertaken simultaneously with another program. Quantitative research applicants should carefully evaluate data availability and accessibility. Qualitative research applicants should clearly explain their data collection methods, including interviews, focus groups, and document reviews. Unsuccessful applicants are encouraged to reapply in future cycles. Application Deadline Application Opens: June 1, 2026 Application Deadline: November 1, 2026 Interested candidates should begin preparing their research proposals, reference letters, and supporting documents well in advance of the deadline. How to Apply Applicants must first create a Harkness Fellowship Portal account before beginning the application process. After registration: Log in to the Harkness Fellowship Portal. Select New User if creating an account for the first time. Complete all required registration fields. Choose Harkness Fellowship Applicant. Complete and submit the online application before the deadline. This fellowship presents an extraordinary opportunity for health policy professionals to contribute to meaningful health system reforms, develop international collaborations, and become part of a prestigious global network of leaders dedicated to improving health care delivery and health equity worldwide. APPLY HERE For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here . The post Commonwealth Fund Harkness Fellowships in Health Care Policy and Practice 2027–2028: Fully Funded Global Fellowship for Health Policy Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: United Nations Development Programme Seoul Policy Centre Internships 2026: Apply for Multiple International Internship Opportunities in Administration, Development Cooperation, Governance, Gender, Communications, and Sustainability
summary: The United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) Seoul Policy Centre is inviting applications from qualified university students, graduate students, and recent graduates for its internship opportunities. These internships provide aspiring professionals with a unique opportunity to gain practical experience within one of the world s leading international development organizations while contributing to initiatives that support sustainable development and global cooperation. Hosted by the UNDP Seoul Policy Centre, these internship opportunities are offered twice annually and are designed to provide participants with meaningful exposure to international development work, policy implementation, governance, sustainability initiatives, communications, and partnership development. Individuals interested in building careers in international development, public administration, governance, sustainability, communications, or partnership management are encouraged to explore these opportunities. About the United Nations Development Programme Seoul Policy Centre The UNDP Seoul Policy Centre serves as a strategic hub that supports development cooperation, knowledge exchange, and South-South and Triangular Cooperation. Through its work, the Centre promotes innovative development solutions and contributes to the achievement of the Sustainable Development Goals. The Centre collaborates with governments, institutions, development partners, and stakeholders worldwide to identify and share effective development policies and practices. Interns at the Centre have the opportunity to work alongside experienced professionals and gain insights into global development processes. Available Internship Positions The current internship opportunities include: 1. Intern – Administration This position provides exposure to the administrative operations that support the Centre s day-to-day activities. Interns may contribute to office management processes, coordination activities, documentation, and operational support functions. 2. Intern – Development Cooperation The Development Cooperation internship offers participants an opportunity to engage with initiatives that promote international development collaboration. Interns may support research, project coordination, knowledge-sharing activities, and development partnerships. 3. Intern – Governance and Gender (2 Positions) The Governance and Gender internship focuses on advancing inclusive governance and gender equality initiatives. Selected interns may contribute to research, policy analysis, programme support, stakeholder engagement, and knowledge management activities. 4. Intern – Private Sector Partnerships This internship provides practical experience in building and managing partnerships with private sector organizations. Interns may assist with partnership development, stakeholder mapping, communications, and collaboration initiatives that support sustainable development objectives. 5. Intern – Communications and Social Media The Communications and Social Media internship is ideal for candidates interested in digital communications, content creation, public engagement, and storytelling. Interns may support communication campaigns, social media management, content development, and outreach activities. 6. Intern – Green Recovery and Transition (2 Positions) The Green Recovery and Transition internship focuses on sustainability, environmental initiatives, and development strategies that support resilient and green economic growth. Interns may contribute to research, programme implementation support, and sustainability-focused projects. Benefits of the UNDP Seoul Policy Centre Internship Participating in a UNDP internship can provide numerous professional and personal development benefits, including: Exposure to international development work. Experience within a globally recognized United Nations agency. Opportunities to work with experts in development policy and practice. Enhanced understanding of sustainable development and global cooperation. Development of professional skills in research, communication, administration, and project support. Networking opportunities with international professionals and stakeholders. Valuable experience that can strengthen future academic and career prospects. Practical understanding of multilateral development institutions and programmes. Why These Internships Stand Out UNDP internships are highly sought after because they offer participants the chance to gain first-hand experience in addressing some of the world s most pressing development challenges. Interns become part of an international environment that values innovation, collaboration, inclusiveness, and sustainable development. The diversity of available internship tracks also allows applicants from various academic and professional backgrounds to identify opportunities aligned with their interests and career goals. Recruitment Cycle The UNDP Seoul Policy Centre offers internships twice every year: Summer recruitment cycle Winter recruitment cycle Recruitment announcements are typically published through the official UNDP Seoul Policy Centre website and social media channels. Interested candidates are encouraged to monitor these platforms regularly for updates and new openings. How to Apply Interested applicants should submit their applications through the official application portal provided by the United Nations Development Programme Seoul Policy Centre. Application Link: https://www.undp.org/seoul/careers Applicants are encouraged to carefully review the specific requirements and responsibilities associated with each internship position before submitting their applications. Deadline No specific application deadline was provided in the announcement. Applications may be reviewed according to the recruitment cycle and vacancy timelines. Interested candidates are strongly encouraged to apply as early as possible and regularly check the official website for updates regarding deadlines and position availability. Final Thoughts The United Nations Development Programme Seoul Policy Centre Internship Programme offers an excellent opportunity for university students, graduate students, and recent graduates seeking international exposure and professional development in the field of sustainable development. With internship opportunities spanning administration, governance, development cooperation, communications, private sector partnerships, and green transition initiatives, applicants can gain valuable experience while contributing to meaningful global development efforts. Prospective candidates should take advantage of this opportunity to develop practical skills, expand their professional networks, and gain insight into the work of one of the world s leading development organizations. APPLY HERE For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Internship Opportunities on OFY here .
